author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xxv. 24, etc., where the slothful servant who would not put out the talent he had received, is condemned.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xxv. 24, etc., where the slothful servant who would not put out the talent he had received, is condemned.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My lords, my brethren, and fellow bishops have deigned to visit us and gladden us by their presence; but I know not why they are unwilling to assist me, when wearied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have said this to you, Beloved, in their hearing, that your hearing may in a manner intercede for me with them, that when I ask them they also may discourse unto you in their turn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them dispense what they have received, let them vouchsafe to work rather than excuse themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be pleased, however, to hear from me, fatigued though I be and have difficulty in speaking, a few words only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we have besides a record of God’s mercies vouchsafed through a holy Martyr, which we must give willing audience to altogether.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it then? what shall I say unto you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard in the Gospel both the due recompense of the good servants, and the punishment of the bad.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the whole wickedness of that servant who was reprobate and severely condemned, was that he would not put out his money to use.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He kept the entire sum he had received; but the Lord looked for profit from it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God is coveteous with regard to our salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he who did not put out to use is so condemned, what must they look for who lose what they have received?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We then are the dispensers, we put out, ye receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We look for profit; do ye live well.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is the profit in our dealings with you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do not think that this office of putting out to use does not belong to you also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye cannot execute it indeed from this elevated seat, but ye can wherever ye chance to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherever Christ is attacked, defend Him; answer murmurers, rebuke blasphemers, from their fellowship keep yourselves apart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So do ye put out to use, if ye make gain of any.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Discharge our office in your own houses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A bishop is called from hence, because he superintends, because he takes care and attends to others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To every man then, if he is the head of his own house, ought the office of the Episcopate to belong, to take care how his household believe, that none of them fall into heresy, neither wife, nor son, nor daughter, nor even his slave, because he has been bought at so great a price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostolic teaching has set the master over the slave, and put the slave under the master; nevertheless Christ gave the same price for both.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not neglect then the least of those belonging to you, look after the salvation of all your household with all vigilance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This if ye do, ye put out to use; ye will not be slothful servants, ye will not have to fear so horrible a condemnation.
